verses.text: 'Let God arise, let his enemies be scattered,' driven off like smoke, while the righteous rejoice exceedingly. His rising means rescue for His own. When God acts, the wicked flee and His people are glad.

'Let God arise, let his enemies be scattered,' driven off like smoke, while the righteous rejoice exceedingly. His rising means rescue for His own. When God acts, the wicked flee and His people are glad.

'Let God arise, let his enemies be scattered,' driven off like smoke, while the righteous rejoice exceedingly. His rising means rescue for His own. When God acts, the wicked flee and His people are glad.

Extol Him who rides upon the heavens, yet is 'a father of the fatherless' who 'setteth the solitary in families.' Majesty and tenderness meet. The high God stoops to care for the lonely and the orphan.

When God went before His people through the wilderness, the earth shook and He sent plentiful rain on His weary inheritance. He led and provided. The God who marched before Israel still goes before His own.

The Lord gave the word, and a great host published it; kings of armies fled before His people. His command brings victory. When God speaks, the mightiest foes scatter.
The Lord gave the word, and a great host published it; kings of armies fled before His people. His command brings victory. When God speaks, the mightiest foes scatter.

He ascended on high, 'led captivity captive,' and received gifts for men. Paul applies this to the risen Christ. Having conquered, the ascended Lord pours out gifts on His Church.

'Blessed be the Lord, who daily loadeth us with benefits,' for to God belong the issues from death. His mercies are daily and life-giving. The God who saves from death heaps good upon us every day.
'Blessed be the Lord, who daily loadeth us with benefits,' for to God belong the issues from death. His mercies are daily and life-giving. The God who saves from death heaps good upon us every day.
God will wound the head of His enemies who persist in their guilt. Judgment awaits the unrepentant. The God of mercy is also the God who breaks the proud.
God will wound the head of His enemies who persist in their guilt. Judgment awaits the unrepentant. The God of mercy is also the God who breaks the proud.

His triumphal procession is seen, singers before and players after, with the maidens and the tribes together. Worship is joyful and ordered. The redeemed of every rank join in praising their King.

He commands His strength for His people, and even Egypt and Ethiopia shall bring tribute and stretch out their hands to God. The nations turn to Him. The kingdom of God draws in peoples once far off.

'Sing unto God, ye kingdoms of the earth,' ascribing strength to Him, for 'the God of Israel is he that giveth strength... unto his people.' All lands are summoned to praise. The God of power is the strength of all who are His.
